verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The first two Magnificent-Seven prints landed and the tape didn’t like them: Alphabet fell after hours on a surge in AI capex and Tesla dropped ~5% on an EPS miss, leaving Nasdaq futures down ~1% and S&P futures off ~0.4%. Oil is still the second engine — WTI pushed to a 6-week high near $88–90, Brent ~$92 — on the ongoing U.S.–Iran conflict, keeping the 10Y near 4.63%. VIX sits near 17.6 and P(≥1% gap) is 34% — just under the 35% trigger, composite 52 (WARM). A live war plus an oil shock plus a disappointing mega-cap open plus INTC/RTX/TMUS tonight lock GREEN out: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and the prints.

composite.band_note: WARM · up from 51 · GOOGL/TSLA disappoint, oil 6-week high

focus.jbig: GOOGL/TSLA sour the tape · oil 6-wk high · INTC tonight · half size
focus.jtag: Alphabet and Tesla disappointed after the close, chip futures slide, oil holds a 6-week high with the Iran conflict still live
focus.jnote: The two forces that ran the tape all month are both still pushing. The first Magnificent-Seven prints landed after Wednesday’s close and the market read them as a disappointment: Alphabet slipped after hours despite a strong quarter and 82% cloud growth, spooked by ~$45B of quarterly capex and negative free cash flow, while Tesla fell about 5% on an adjusted-EPS miss. The second force is oil — WTI rose more than 2% Wednesday to a six-week high and climbed further overnight as the U.S.–Iran conflict grinds on and a surprise US crude inventory build did little to cool it. The 10Y sits near 4.63%, gold slipped toward $4,092, and futures point lower (S&P ~−0.4%, Nasdaq ~−1%). Intel, RTX and T-Mobile report tonight, with jobless claims and the ECB decision today. This is a live, headline-and-earnings tape — deploy carefully, not aggressively.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~17.6, elevated; closed ~17.24 Wed, ticking up pre-open on soft Mag-7 futures; 52-wk range 13.38–35.30
Big events (next 1–3 days) — INTC/RTX/TMUS earnings TONIGHT AMC, jobless claims & ECB today, flash PMIs Fri; Fed July 29 ahead
World risk — U.S.–Iran conflict still live, Gulf/Red Sea shipping risk keeping an oil bid; no clean de-escalation yet
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; curve sensitive to any oil shock or a soft chip read from Intel tonight
Commodities — oil at a 6-week high, WTI ~$88–90 on Hormuz/Red Sea risk; still the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: +~2% · elevated · ticking up on soft futures

setups.read: The oil supply shock is still real, durable news — crude sits at a six-week high and energy keeps catching a supply-fear bid while the Iran conflict grinds on. But this remains a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The whole move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline gaps crude back $5–7 in minutes, taking your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue for the ETF, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only. (XOM reports Fri 7/24 — check the single names.)
setups.check_note: On a YELLOW day, don’t chase a war premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Chips are wobbling again after Alphabet’s capex spooked the AI-infrastructure trade and the SOX has fallen sharply off its late-June high — so the near-term lean is down. But Intel and Texas Instruments report tonight/this week, dropping earnings risk right into any DTE, and selling a bear call into a VIX-elevated tape invites a violent squeeze on any upbeat guidance. This is exactly the print-inside-DTE blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Earnings tonight + squeeze risk = no. Don’t carry a chip spread through the INTC/TXN prints.

setups.read: Jet fuel is at a six-week high with crude near $90, a direct margin headwind — so the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: any diplomacy/ceasefire headline squeezes shorts violently, and VIX near 17.6 makes a bear call a snap-back hazard. Selling a short-call into a live-war, VIX-elevated tape is not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm each name’s earnings date before any DTE.
setups.check_note: Diplomacy-squeeze risk into a fuel headwind = wait. Don’t chase the short until the tape settles.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this Thursday morning that qualifies. GOOGL slipped and TSLA fell ~5% after the close — both moved down on the print into a jumpy war-and-oil tape, and neither is a clean Sniper: Tesla is a miss, Alphabet a ‘good quarter, scary capex’ sell-off, not a durable double-beat re-rate. The bigger chip prints (INTC/TXN/RTX/TMUS) haven’t hit yet. Check back Friday once tonight’s reports land.
setups.check_note: Nothing clean to validate — no qualifying Type A candidate today.

setups.read: A soft-Mag-7, oil-shock, futures-down morning is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts. With the Nasdaq well off its late-June high and no fresh high-volume ATH breakout holding into a GOOGL/TSLA-disappointment, pre-chip-earnings open, there’s nothing to lean on. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-and-earnings chop, soft tech
scenarios.sub: Futures stay soft on the GOOGL/TSLA read and oil; chips whipsaw, the tape lurches on Iran/oil headlines into tonight’s INTC/RTX/TMUS prints, vol elevated

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation / weak chip read
scenarios.sub: Strikes intensify or Hormuz/Red Sea shipping is disrupted and/or Intel guides soft; oil runs toward $95, chips extend the selloff, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation + strong chip prints
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire firms up and INTC/TXN beat; oil gives back the spike, a relief bounce in tech and chips, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran / Strait of Hormuz / Red Sea — the U.S.–Iran conflict is still live with oil at a six-week high on supply-disruption fear. Any wider strike or shipping attack sends crude and jet fuel vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

INTC / RTX / TMUS earnings TONIGHT (AMC) — Intel is the key chip read after Alphabet’s capex spooked the AI-infrastructure trade; a soft foundry or guidance print could deepen the semiconductor selloff and gap the tech tape. Keep every spread clear of each name’s print.

GOOGL/TSLA aftermath — Alphabet’s ~$45B quarterly capex and negative free cash flow, plus Tesla’s EPS miss, have already knocked Nasdaq futures ~−1%; follow-through selling in mega-cap tech is the near-term drag.

Oil near $90 & the 10-year — WTI at a six-week high keeps inflation risk alive; the 10Y is near a two-month high around 4.63%, and a rate pop compounds pressure on stocks into the July 29 Fed decision.
Jobless claims & ECB today · flash PMIs Fri 7/24 — macro catalysts stacked on the war and earnings. A hot claims number or weak PMI would compound the risk.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is deep in mega-cap week — Alphabet and Tesla reported Wednesday after the close (both fell), and Intel, RTX and T-Mobile report tonight (Thu 7/23) after the bell. With the Iran conflict still live and oil at a six-week high, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Foundry progress, data-center and PC-chip demand, and margins are the swing factors, with consensus near $0.21 EPS on ~$14.4B revenue. Lands right after Alphabet’s capex spooked the AI-infrastructure trade and with the SOX well off its late-June high — any soft guidance could reignite the chip selloff.
rows.angle: Do not hold any chip spread through tonight’s INTC print. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Friday.

rows.why: RTX carries conflict-demand tailwinds but a two-sided peace-headline risk; T-Mobile hinges on wireless subscriber adds and free cash flow. Both drop single-stock gap risk into an already headline-jumpy tape.
rows.angle: Keep any defense/telecom spread to a DTE that clears the print — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail.

rows.why: ExxonMobil is directly leveraged to the oil spike; American Express reads the high-income consumer and credit quality. Both land the same morning as the flash PMIs, stacking macro and single-stock risk.
rows.angle: Any energy or financials spread must expire before its print — and mind the PMI overlap Friday.

earnings.outro: Thu 7/23: the tape trades the GOOGL/TSLA disappointment, oil and Iran headlines into tonight’s INTC/RTX/TMUS prints, alongside jobless claims and the ECB decision. It stays real Friday with XOM and AmEx plus the flash PMIs, then the Fed on July 29. Half size, A-grade only, DTE clear of every print, until the tape settles.
